summary: Matched several small functions in src/melee/ft/ftdata.c, raising ftdata.c to 20.79% matched over 2.92 KB. The PR added concrete implementations for ftData_80085560, ftData_800855C8, ftData_80085B10, and ftData_80086060, refined ftdata-related data-table shapes, exposed needed prototypes and struct fields, and marked the corresponding scratch entries OK. No review comments were present in the provided slice.
what_changed: Added implementations in ftdata.c for ftData_80085560, ftData_800855C8, ftData_80085B10, and ftData_80086060.

Introduced global int ft_8045996C[FTKIND_MAX] used by ftData_80085560 as a fighter reference counter; the function increments by an argument and asserts with OSReport/HSD_ASSERT if the counter becomes negative.

Implemented ftData_800855C8 to validate costume slot selection, queue fighter data and costume .dat loads with lbDvd_800178E8, call efAsync_8006729C when ftData_UnkBytePerCharacter[id] is valid, and queue animation .dat loads from ftData_803C23E4.
Implemented ftData_80085B10 to allocate Fighter fields x59C and x5A0 with HSD_ObjAlloc, initialize x5A4/x5A8 to NULL, set x58C from ftData_Table_Unk0[kind].count, and call ftData_80085A14(kind).

Implemented ftData_80086060 to return an existing Fighter pointer for a specific arg0->unk4 == 11 case when Player_GetPlayerSlotType(arg0->unkC) != 2 and Player_GetEntityAtIndex returns a non-NULL GObj; otherwise it returns NULL.
Changed ftData_803C1F40 from a flat char* array of FTKIND_MAX * 2 into an array of struct StringPair with fields a and b, matching the code's per-fighter dat filename/data-name pair access pattern.

Split a previous anonymous aggregate ftData_803C2360 containing costume string pointers and animation dat filenames into two separate arrays: Fighter_CostumeStrings* ftData_803C2360[FTKIND_MAX] and char* ftData_803C23E4[FTKIND_MAX].
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt so ftData_803C2360 has size 0x84 and added a new ftData_803C23E4 object at .data:0x803C23E4 with size 0x84.
Added struct ftData_80086060_arg0 to ftdata.h and changed ftData_80086060 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to Fighter* ftData_80086060(struct ftData_80086060_arg0* arg0).
Changed ftData_80085560's prototype from void ftData_80085560(s32, bool) to void ftData_80085560(int idx, int increment).
Refined Fighter fields in types.h around offsets 0x5A4 and 0x5A8 from filler into explicit UNK_T x5A4 and UNK_T x5A8 fields.
Updated ftlib.c to use CostumeListsForeachCharacter[arg].numCostumes instead of an ad hoc extern with field unk4, relying on the proper definition from ft/ft_0852.h.
Marked scratch entries OK for func_8008521C, ft_8008521C, func_80085560, func_800855C8, func_80085B10, and func_80086060.
smart_moves: Recognized that ftData_803C2360 was not one 0x108-byte struct object but two adjacent 0x84-byte arrays, then fixed both C declarations and symbols.txt to reflect the actual data layout.
Converted the standard character dat-name table into a small StringPair array, making ftData_803C1F40[id].b access natural and avoiding error-prone flat index arithmetic.
Removed a local anonymous extern for CostumeListsForeachCharacter in ftlib.c and included ft/ft_0852.h instead, allowing use of the semantically named numCostumes field.
Advanced matching by first exposing missing dependencies through includes and prototypes: efAsync_8006729C, lbDvd, player accessors, fighter inlines, HSD_ObjAlloc, and debug/assert support.
Used observed field offsets in Fighter to replace broad filler with explicit x5A4 and x5A8 members needed by the matched allocation/init routine.
Kept scratch metadata in sync with the newly matched functions by changing relevant entries from percentages to OK.
decomp_lessons: When an apparent struct-of-arrays object causes awkward access or symbol size mismatches, verify whether the linker data is actually multiple adjacent arrays; splitting objects can unlock cleaner matching.
For tables that repeat fixed pairs per fighter, declaring an explicit pair struct can better model the generated code than a flat pointer array.
Prefer importing the real header that defines shared data structures over declaring local anonymous externs; this improves naming, field accuracy, and reuse across files.
Small matched functions often require nearby type/header cleanup: adding one function body may expose missing prototypes, missing global declarations, and incomplete struct fields.
Scratch and symbol files should be updated together with C matching changes so object boundaries and decomp status remain searchable and consistent.
Counter/allocator initialization functions can reveal real Fighter struct fields; use concrete writes in matched code to replace filler gradually.
naming_conventions: Existing unmatched or partially understood functions retain address-based names such as ftData_80085560, ftData_800855C8, ftData_80085B10, and ftData_80086060.
The PR uses subsystem prefix casing already present in the project: ftData_ for fighter data functions/objects and efAsync_ for effect async functions.
Anonymous or unknown data keeps address-based names such as ftData_803C2360 and ftData_803C23E4 when semantics are only partially known.
New temporary struct names can be address/function-scoped when the real type is unknown, as with struct ftData_80086060_arg0.
Known fields should replace generic names where evidence exists, as shown by using CostumeListsForeachCharacter[arg].numCostumes instead of unk4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Modeled adjacent .data objects by changing ftData_803C2360 from size 0x108 to size 0x84 and adding ftData_803C23E4 at the next address, matching the symbol layout.
Used exact-width local temporaries in ftData_800855C8, including u8 variables for costume slot iteration, likely to match register behavior and comparisons.
Preserved sentinel behavior for costume slot 0xFF in ftData_800855C8: 0xFF expands to all costumes, while out-of-range non-0xFF slots are clamped to 0.
Matched pointer-table code by changing ftData_803C1F40 access from flat char* indexing to struct field access.
Matched ftData_80086060 control flow by checking arg0->unk4 == 11, Player_GetPlayerSlotType(arg0->unkC) != 2, null-checking the retrieved GObj, and returning GET_FIGHTER(gobj) or NULL.
Used explicit offset fields x5A4 and x5A8 in Fighter so assignments to NULL land at the correct structure offsets.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename ftData_803C2360 or ftData_803C23E4 to semantic names for costume strings and animation dat filenames.
Search for follow-up work on ftData_800855C8 to improve its match percentage or clarify its local variable types.
Find PRs that further type struct ftData_80086060_arg0 or identify the meaning of fields unk4 and unkC.
Check whether ft_8045996C later receives a semantic name tied to fighter reference counting.
Search later changes to Fighter fields around offsets 0x59C through 0x5A8 for refined types beyond UNK_T.
